pi_items.source: setup_task.sh injects the initial state memo exactly to this path.
export_result.sh explicitly checks this exact path for the expected report.
Python simulation of J2 RAAN precession and Sun ephemeris confirms the maximum beta angle over 180 days starting 2026-01-01 is ~71.5 degrees, falling within the [68.0, 76.0] tolerance.
Python simulation of J2 RAAN precession and Sun ephemeris confirms the maximum beta angle over 180 days starting 2026-01-01 is ~71.5 degrees, falling within the [68.0, 76.0] tolerance.
Python simulation of beta angle over 180 days confirms there are ~14 days where abs(beta) > 60.0 degrees, falling within the [10, 25] tolerance.
Python simulation of beta angle over 180 days confirms there are ~14 days where abs(beta) > 60.0 degrees, falling within the [10, 25] tolerance.
The task description requires exactly 180 days, and verifier.py checks that the propagation duration is >= 179.0.

privileged_info_summary: The task dynamically generates a synthetic ISS orbital state memo at '/home/ga/Desktop/iss_state_memo.txt' for an epoch of 1 Jan 2026. Independent astrodynamics simulation of this orbit under Earth's J2 oblateness reveals a maximum beta angle of approximately 71.5 degrees and roughly 14 days spent above a 60-degree beta angle over the 180-day propagation period. These true values are firmly bounded by the verifier's allowable ranges of [68.0, 76.0] for max beta and [10, 25] for high beta days. The expected report path is '/home/ga/GMAT_output/iss_beta_report.txt'.
